skill.name: architecture-reference
skill.description: Quick reference for Portfolio Buddy 2 project structure. Use when: adding new features, modifying existing components, understanding data flow, or onboarding to the codebase. Contains component hierarchy, hook patterns, and utility functions.
skill.summary: Reference guide for Portfolio Buddy components, hooks, data flow, and refactoring patterns.
skill.author: 5MinFutures

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, inline code identifiers, and architecture notes in SKILL.md. No executable script, command invocation, weak cryptographic implementation, prompt injection, data exfiltration, secret access, or destructive behavior was found. The skill is documentation-only and is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown Reference Text
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The external command alerts point to Markdown code fences, TypeScript examples, inline function names, and architecture diagrams. These lines document React hooks, utility functions, and project structure; they do not instruct the agent to execute shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are visibly Markdown fences or inline TypeScript examples. There is no shell syntax, subprocess API, or instruction to run commands.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Textual Matches Only
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts do not correspond to any cryptographic implementation. The referenced lines describe the skill, sorting behavior, metric names, data interfaces, and callback examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain no MD5, SHA-1, crypto library usage, random token generation, or authentication logic. The alerts are substring matches in documentation.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are Architecture Documentation
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The system and network reconnaissance alerts map to prose about project state, Supabase data flow, and development guidance. No scanner, network probing command, host enumeration, or environment discovery instruction is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines are documentation bullets and coding guidance. I found no commands such as nmap, netcat, ipconfig, whoami, or similar reconnaissance tooling.

content.value_statement: Portfolio Buddy changes require quick context about components, hooks, and data flow. This skill gives a concise project map for safer feature work.
actual_capabilities: Explains the Portfolio Buddy 2 component hierarchy and major UI sections.
Summarizes custom hooks for metrics, portfolio filtering, contract multipliers, and sorting.
Documents data flow from CSV upload through Supabase storage and analytics rendering.
Identifies utility functions for parsing, formatting, correlations, and trading calculations.
Highlights refactoring targets, TypeScript patterns, and Chart.js usage constraints.
limitations: It is a static architecture reference, not a live code analyzer.
It focuses on Portfolio Buddy 2 and may not fit unrelated projects.
It does not validate current source files after repository changes.
It does not execute tests or verify runtime behavior.

use_cases.title: Onboard to Portfolio Buddy
use_cases.description: Use the reference to learn the app layout, data path, and main hooks before editing code.

use_cases.title: Plan a Metrics Feature
use_cases.description: Find where metric calculations, tables, sorting, and chart updates should be changed.

use_cases.title: Scope Refactoring Work
use_cases.description: Review component size warnings and extraction targets before splitting large React components.

prompt_templates.title: Find the Right File
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner orientation before a small change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the architecture reference to tell me which Portfolio Buddy files are relevant for adding a new displayed metric.

prompt_templates.title: Trace Data Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Understand how uploaded data reaches the UI.
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain the Portfolio Buddy upload and processing flow from CSV upload to metrics display.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Hook Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the architecture reference to plan changes for date-filtered portfolio analytics without introducing global state.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Refactor
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a refactoring plan for PortfolioSection using the documented component size guidelines and Chart.js constraints.

output_examples.input: Where should I add a new portfolio statistic?
output_examples.output: Start with the metrics utility and the portfolio section. Then update the table column, sorting behavior, and chart display if needed.

output_examples.input: How does a CSV upload become an equity curve?
output_examples.output: The file is parsed, cleaned, uploaded, fetched by the portfolio hook, filtered by date, converted into metrics, and rendered in charts.
Prioritize the large portfolio section. Extract chart rendering, statistics, and contract controls while keeping orchestration in the parent component.

best_practices: Use the reference before editing unfamiliar Portfolio Buddy components or hooks.
Confirm source files still match the reference before making large changes.
Keep new work aligned with the documented React hooks, TypeScript, and Chart.js patterns.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the reference as proof that the current repository is unchanged.
Do not introduce global state libraries when the reference calls for local hooks and props.
Do not add Recharts-based charts when the project standard is Chart.js.

faq.question: What project does this skill describe?
faq.answer: It describes Portfolio Buddy 2, a React and TypeScript application for portfolio analytics.

faq.question: Can it modify code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides architecture context that an AI assistant or developer can use while editing code.

faq.question: Does it run commands or scripts?
faq.answer: No. The skill contains documentation only and does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: It is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it replace reading source files?
faq.answer: No. Use it for orientation, then inspect current source files before implementation.

faq.question: What are the main topics covered?
faq.answer: It covers components, hooks, utility functions, data flow, Chart.js usage, TypeScript patterns, and refactoring targets.
